module: layer (canvas) + Annotation Cue widget dropped into the LoD column
qcSource: Author Test Cases - LoD and Annottaion cue.pdf

verified.name: LoD
verified.dropMechanism: LoD is a Layer (not an assessment widget) → resolveDropTarget routes it to the canvas (#desktop_view) via cy.dropComponentForModule. The LoD column accepts ONLY annotationCueHighlightOutline (LoD-directive.js onDropComplete returns early otherwise); the cue is injected through that scope method.

architectureNotes: config.js gates BOTH widgets off in the layout droppability map ("lod":false, "annotationCueHighlightOutline":false) — they are flag-gated, so every drop-dependent test logs + returns gracefully when the widget is absent from scope.widgets.
LoD canvas wrapper = .LoD-container.layer-lod ([lod-template]); host .customClass carries a unique lod-id ('lod-' + Date.now()) stamped once at first drop (LoD-directive.js).

Display Criteria groups (Level of Support / Type / Activity) are loaded from a backend API (fetchLODSettingData); displayCritaria is [] by default — so all value-level QC cases (CORE/LIGHT/MODERATE/INTENSIVE, Activity 'Add New', tag persistence/DB sync) require a provisioned backend and are deferred.

Link Activity: input #customLinkedActivity + add icon .lod-activity-add-container.icon-Link; validateAndAddLink() rejects empty and duplicate Ref IDs; list rows .activity-list with .lod-activity-list-delete; Apply Tag = #apply-tag-button (disabled until isApplyEnabled).

Annotation Cue: default title 'Stop and Annotate'; editable title .cue-section-title-text + body .cue-section-textarea; non-editable pen icon .icon-Highlight_AnnotationCue; settings colour input .colorPickerInputBox (maxlength 7, default #6E35C8).

CLP/Reader rendering, Show/Hide cues, Publish/republish, persistence-after-reload, undo/redo, cross-device, a11y, RTE-toolbar formatting, file uploads, clipboard paste into layouts/multipart, and EPUB project bootstrap all run outside the authoring DOM → deferred.
Selectors derived from source templates but NOT live-verified (feature gated off by default). Graceful 'ℹ' log paths are expected until the flag is enabled.
